_meta.description: ENEMY EXPANSION — adds ~140 new enemies across all books, bringing total from 144 to 280+. Supplements enemies-complete.json.
_meta.design_note: Classic JRPGs have 25-30 enemy types per zone. Books 3-8 were light. This expansion fills every book to 25-30 and adds moral depth — enemies who are pitiable, enemies who can be reasoned with, enemies who are genuinely evil, and enemies who are just animals doing what animals do.

new_enemies.name: Paddy Snake
new_enemies.description: Harmless unless you step on it. You stepped on it.

new_enemies.name: Crow Flock
new_enemies.description: Black wings, black eyes. They follow death. They've learned what burning chapels mean.

new_enemies.name: Angry Farmer
new_enemies.description: Not a soldier. Just a man who blames Christians for the trouble in his village. He might be right.

new_enemies.name: Tax Collector
new_enemies.description: Takes everything from Christian families. Not cruel — just efficient. The cruelty is the system.

demon_form.name: Imp of Greed
demon_form.whisper: It's not stealing. It's the law.

new_enemies.name: Mountain Hawk
new_enemies.description: Talons like razors. Defends its nest on the mountain path.

new_enemies.name: Wild Dog Pack
new_enemies.description: Village dogs gone feral after the burnings. They remember people. They don't trust them anymore.

new_enemies.name: Drunken Ashigaru
new_enemies.description: Celebrating the raid. Sake on his breath. He swings wide. Easy to dodge. Hard to forgive.

new_enemies.name: Magistrate's Clerk
new_enemies.description: Carries a list of names. Every Christian in the village. His brush is deadlier than any sword.

new_enemies.name: Shinto Temple Guardian
new_enemies.description: Guards the local shrine. Sees Christianity as foreign corruption. His faith is sincere. So is yours.

new_enemies.name: Mountain Mudslide
new_enemies.description: Rain loosened the mountainside. The path is gone.

new_enemies.name: Takeshi (Childhood Friend)
new_enemies.description: You grew up together. He joined the ashigaru because his family needed the rice. He doesn't want to fight you. But if you won't renounce, he has to bring you in.

new_enemies.name: Rat Swarm
new_enemies.description: Hundreds of them. Individual rats are nothing. The swarm is something.

new_enemies.name: Drunken Legionary
new_enemies.description: Off duty. Wine-soaked. Still wearing his armor. Still dangerous.

new_enemies.name: Arena Wolf
new_enemies.description: Captured in Germania. Starved for the arena. Its howl echoes off marble.

new_enemies.name: Arena Bear
new_enemies.description: Chained. Muzzled. Released. It doesn't know why it's fighting. It only knows it's afraid.

new_enemies.name: Publicanus
new_enemies.description: Tax collector. Will inform on Christians for a fee. Will also look the other way for a larger fee.

new_enemies.name: Vigiles (Fire Watch)
new_enemies.description: Rome's firefighters. During the Great Fire they're everywhere. They're also looking for arsonists — and Nero says Christians are arsonists.

new_enemies.name: Priest of Jupiter
new_enemies.description: Sees Christians as atheists — they deny the gods. From his perspective, he's defending civilization.

demon_form.name: Idol Spirit
demon_form.whisper: These gods are as real as yours. Aren't they?

new_enemies.name: Roman Mob
new_enemies.description: Twenty citizens who heard Christians burned Rome. They need someone to blame. You'll do.

new_enemies.name: Praetorian Decurion
new_enemies.description: Commands ten Praetorians. The plume on his helmet is red. He earned it.

new_enemies.name: Demas's Disciple
new_enemies.description: Follows the companion who abandoned Paul. Teaches that the flesh doesn't matter — so do whatever you want with it. Attractive theology. Wrong theology.

new_enemies.name: Tunnel Collapse
new_enemies.description: The catacombs are old. The stone is older. Sometimes gravity wins.

new_enemies.name: Wall of Fire
new_enemies.description: The Great Fire of Rome. It burned for six days. There is no fighting this. Only running.

new_enemies.name: Onesimus (Runaway Slave)
new_enemies.description: A runaway slave hiding in the catacombs. He thinks you're a bounty hunter. He will fight to the death rather than go back. He shouldn't have to.

new_enemies.name: Actor in Nero's Mask
new_enemies.description: Wears Nero's golden mask at the arena games. Possibly Nero himself. Possibly an actor. The crowd can't tell. The effect is the same.

new_enemies.name: Scarab Beetle Swarm
new_enemies.description: Sacred to pagans. Just hungry to everyone else.

new_enemies.name: Desert Fox
new_enemies.description: Fennec fox. Enormous ears, fast feet. Steals food from camp.

new_enemies.name: Egyptian Vulture
new_enemies.description: Circles overhead. Patient. It knows what the desert does to travelers.

new_enemies.name: Nile Hippopotamus
new_enemies.description: The most dangerous animal in Egypt. Not the crocodile. Not the lion. This.

new_enemies.name: Arian Street Preacher
new_enemies.description: Preaches in the Alexandria market. Christ is created, not begotten, he says. The crowd likes the simplicity of it.

new_enemies.name: Arian Monk
new_enemies.description: Desert monk who follows the Arian doctrine. His asceticism is genuine. His theology is not.

new_enemies.name: Roman Garrison Soldier
new_enemies.description: Roman soldier stationed in Egypt. Enforces whatever bishop the emperor currently supports.

new_enemies.name: Alexandrian Scholar
new_enemies.description: Scholar from the Great Library's remnants. Attacks with arguments, not swords. More dangerous.

demon_form.name: Imp of Pride
demon_form.whisper: You believe in fairy tales. I deal in facts.

new_enemies.name: Tomb Robber
new_enemies.description: Robs pharaonic tombs. Will rob Christian holy sites with equal enthusiasm. God is not relevant to his profession.

new_enemies.name: Dust Devil
new_enemies.description: Miniature sandstorm. Blinds you. Steals your map. The desert plays tricks.

new_enemies.name: Desert Heat
new_enemies.description: 50 degrees. The air shimmers. Water is a memory. Prayer is a lifeline.

new_enemies.name: Vision of Luxury
new_enemies.description: The desert shows you what you want most. Cold water. A bed. A woman's voice. None of it is real. All of it is you.

new_enemies.name: Priest of Serapis
new_enemies.description: The old Egyptian gods still have followers. He sees Christians as the new threat to the old order.

demon_form.name: Idol Spirit
demon_form.whisper: These temples stood for three thousand years before your Christ.

new_enemies.name: Athanasius's Bodyguard
new_enemies.description: Loyal to the Nicene bishop. Fights Arians on sight. On your side — unless you're confused for an Arian.

new_enemies.name: False Hermit
new_enemies.description: Claims to be a Desert Father. Has been alone so long the desert has eaten his mind. His prophecies sound true. They're reflections of your own fears.

demon_form.name: Shade of Isolation
demon_form.whisper: God speaks to me alone. Not to the Church. To ME.

new_enemies.name: Arian Assassin
new_enemies.description: Sent to kill a Nicene bishop before the Council. Professional. No ideology — just a job. But the man who hired him has ideology enough for both.

new_enemies.name: Siege Rat
new_enemies.description: Fat on corpses. The siege has been good to the rats.

new_enemies.name: Starving Camp Dog
new_enemies.description: Belonged to a soldier. The soldier is dead. The dog is hungry. You feel terrible fighting it.

new_enemies.name: Scorpion Nest
new_enemies.description: Under the rubble. Dozens of them. The siege shook them out of hiding.

new_enemies.name: Crusader Looter
new_enemies.description: Wearing the cross. Looting the dead. Both sides' dead. He doesn't care.

demon_form.name: Imp of Greed
demon_form.whisper: They're dead. They don't need it. You do.

new_enemies.name: Mamluk Commander
new_enemies.description: Brilliant tactician. Commands a hundred men with hand signals. Fights with the grace of a dancer.

new_enemies.name: Hospitaller Medic (Corrupted)
new_enemies.description: Once healed the sick. The siege broke something in him. Now he decides who deserves healing and who doesn't.

new_enemies.name: Boy With a Sword
new_enemies.description: Twelve years old. Sword too big for him. Father died on the walls. He picked it up. You CANNOT kill this child.

new_enemies.name: Boiling Oil
new_enemies.description: Poured from the walls. Clings. Burns through armor. The smell is the worst part.

new_enemies.name: Trebuchet Bombardment
new_enemies.description: Stones the size of oxen. Falling from the sky. You can't fight gravity.

new_enemies.name: Venetian Arms Dealer
new_enemies.description: Sells weapons to both sides. Has no loyalty except to gold. Venice profits from the siege.

demon_form.name: Imp of Greed
demon_form.whisper: War is business. Business is good.

new_enemies.name: Mamluk Berserker
new_enemies.description: Fights without armor. Fights without fear. Has decided today is a good day to die.

new_enemies.name: Fire Barrel Bombardment
new_enemies.description: Barrels of pitch launched over the walls. When they hit, everything burns.

new_enemies.name: Leper Knight
new_enemies.description: Knight who contracted leprosy. Still fighting. No one will stand near him. He fights alone. He prays alone.

new_enemies.name: Bishop Militant
new_enemies.description: Bishop in full armor. Carries a mace instead of a sword — technically not shedding blood. He has convinced himself that killing for Christ is Christ's will. He is wrong. He is dangerous. He is not entirely evil.

demon_form.name: War Demon Fragment
demon_form.whisper: God needs soldiers. Be His sword.

new_enemies.name: Ibn Rashid (Scholar-Warrior)
new_enemies.description: Muslim scholar who fights as well as he writes. Quotes Rumi in combat. Asks genuine theological questions between sword strokes. A worthy opponent in every sense.

new_enemies.name: Steppe Eagle
new_enemies.description: Golden eagle of the steppe. Wingspan wider than a man is tall.

new_enemies.name: Panicked Wild Horse
new_enemies.description: Horse freed from a dead rider. Panicked by fire and blood. Not aggressive. Just terrified.

new_enemies.name: Mongolian War Dog
new_enemies.description: Tibetan mastiff bred for war. Massive. Loyal. Follows the Mongol army.

new_enemies.name: Mongol Mangudai
new_enemies.description: Light cavalry skirmisher. Fires from horseback at full gallop, then vanishes. You cannot catch him. You can only endure.

new_enemies.name: Tengrist Shaman
new_enemies.description: Follows Tengri, the Eternal Blue Sky. His spirits are real — or at least, something answers when he calls.

demon_form.name: Steppe Spirit
demon_form.whisper: Your God is one. Our spirits are many. Numbers matter.

new_enemies.name: Chinese Siege Engineer
new_enemies.description: Captured and enslaved by the Mongols. His knowledge of gunpowder is centuries ahead of Europe. He doesn't want to be here.

new_enemies.name: Polish Village Militia
new_enemies.description: Farmer with a spear. His wife and children are behind him. He will not run.

new_enemies.name: Burning Village
new_enemies.description: The Mongols burn everything they pass. The village is fuel. The screaming is the sound.

new_enemies.name: Frozen River Crossing
new_enemies.description: The river looks solid. It is not. Every step might be the last.

new_enemies.name: Mongol Prisoner Guard
new_enemies.description: Guards a line of captured Polish civilians. If you fight him, he might kill a prisoner first.

new_enemies.name: Polish Knight of Krakow
new_enemies.description: Heavy cavalry. He knows this is the last charge. He charges anyway.

new_enemies.name: Mongol Wrestler-Champion
new_enemies.description: Mongol bökh wrestler. Seven feet tall. Fights without weapons. Doesn't need them.

new_enemies.name: Duke Henry's Ghost
new_enemies.description: The ghost of Henry the Pious, who died at Legnica. He charges one more time. Not against you — alongside you. But first you must prove you're worthy of his company.

new_enemies.name: Shadow of Subutai
new_enemies.description: The greatest military mind in human history. 60 years old and still fights from the saddle. He conquered more territory than Alexander, Caesar, and Napoleon combined. He does not hate you. He has never hated anyone he killed.

new_enemies.name: Mountain Boar Alpha
new_enemies.description: Massive. Scarred. Rules this mountain. Doesn't care about your religion.

new_enemies.name: Tanuki
new_enemies.description: Japanese raccoon dog. Steals your food. Looks guilty about it.

new_enemies.name: Japanese Macaque
new_enemies.description: Throws rocks from the trees. Travels in troops. Individually harmless, collectively annoying.

new_enemies.name: Village Headman
new_enemies.description: Responsible for reporting Christians. If one is found in his village, his entire family is punished. He informs to protect his children. Can you blame him?

new_enemies.name: Buddhist Temple Inspector
new_enemies.description: Checks that every household is registered at a Buddhist temple. His job is to make Christians visible. His thoroughness is legendary.

new_enemies.name: Tokugawa Cavalry
new_enemies.description: Heavy horse. Lance. Speed. If they find the hidden village, it's over in minutes.

new_enemies.name: Gun Ashigaru Squad
new_enemies.description: Three muskets firing in rotation. One always loaded. The age of the sword is ending.

new_enemies.name: Kakure Traitor
new_enemies.description: Hidden Christian who was caught. Under torture, revealed the hidden chapel's location. Weeps the entire fight. Attacks because the soldiers are watching.

demon_form.name: Shade of Despair
demon_form.whisper: They broke me. Wouldn't they break you?

new_enemies.name: Shimabara Castle Defender
new_enemies.description: Christian rebel defending the last castle. He knows they will lose. Every day of resistance is a prayer.

new_enemies.name: Ghost of Fr. Rodrigues
new_enemies.description: The ghost of a priest who stepped on the fumi-e. He asks: did he apostatize, or did he hear Christ say 'Step'? There is no right answer. That's the point.

new_enemies.name: Amakusa Shirō (Vision)
new_enemies.description: Vision of the teenage leader of the Shimabara Rebellion. Sixteen years old. Led 37,000 Christians. All died. He appears and asks: 'Was it worth it?' The game changes based on your answer.

new_enemies.name: Inoue's Torturer
new_enemies.description: The man who actually operates the pit. He doesn't enjoy it. He's found that not enjoying it makes him better at it. He offers you a way out every turn. All you have to do is step on the image.

new_enemies.name: Mediterranean Shark
new_enemies.description: Drawn by the blood of 40,000 dead. The sea battle has turned the Mediterranean red.

new_enemies.name: Scavenging Gulls
new_enemies.description: Hundreds of gulls feeding on the dead in the water. They attack anything that moves.

new_enemies.name: Ship Fire
new_enemies.description: The galley is on fire. The tar, the wood, the sails — all burning. Jump or die.

new_enemies.name: Sinking Ship
new_enemies.description: The galley is going down. The deck tilts. Everything slides. The water rises.

new_enemies.name: Ottoman Marine
new_enemies.description: Trained for ship-to-ship combat. Fights in tight spaces. Boarding axe in one hand, shield in the other.

new_enemies.name: Ottoman War Drummer
new_enemies.description: His drum keeps the rowers' rhythm. When he plays faster, the galley charges. Kill the drummer, slow the ship.

new_enemies.name: Venetian Intelligence Agent
new_enemies.description: Venetian spy. Fights with a poisoned ring and information. Both are lethal.

new_enemies.name: Confused Freed Slave
new_enemies.description: Christian slave freed when the galley was captured. Swinging chains at everything that moves. Can't tell friend from foe in the smoke.

new_enemies.name: Genoese Crossbowman
new_enemies.description: Italian mercenary crossbowman. Fires from behind a pavise shield. One bolt punches through armor.

new_enemies.name: Galley Cannon
new_enemies.description: Bow cannon on a galley. One shot. Devastating. Then four turns to reload. Rush it.

new_enemies.name: Don John's Champion
new_enemies.description: The personal champion of Don John of Austria. Fights on the deck of the Real. His swordsmanship is legendary. He fights with a rosary wrapped around his sword hand.

new_enemies.name: Drowned Oarsmen
new_enemies.description: Ghosts of the chained oarsmen who drowned when galley sank. Still chained. Still rowing. They don't want to fight you. They want to be unchained.

new_enemies.name: Parisian Stray Cat
new_enemies.description: Lives in the sewers. Scratches if cornered. Otherwise just watches with yellow eyes.

new_enemies.name: Paris Sewer Crocodile
new_enemies.description: Yes, there are crocodiles in the Paris sewers. No one knows how they got there.

new_enemies.name: The Guillotine
new_enemies.description: Not an enemy you fight. An enemy you escape. Three turns to break free before the blade falls.

new_enemies.name: Revolutionary Drummer Boy
new_enemies.description: Fourteen years old. Drums the march. Believes in Liberty with his whole heart. You cannot kill this child.

new_enemies.name: Committee of Public Safety Agent
new_enemies.description: Robespierre's eyes. He watches everyone. He trusts no one. His lists grow longer every day.

demon_form.name: Imp of Pride
demon_form.whisper: Virtue requires vigilance. Vigilance requires terror.

new_enemies.name: Market Woman (Enragée)
new_enemies.description: Her children are starving. She blames priests and aristocrats. She marched on Versailles. She is not wrong about everything.

new_enemies.name: National Guard Officer
new_enemies.description: Officer of the Revolutionary army. Educated, principled, and absolutely willing to shoot a priest for the Republic.

new_enemies.name: Street Urchin Gang
new_enemies.description: Orphans of the Revolution. Steal to eat. Some inform for bread. None of them chose this.

new_enemies.name: Actress as Goddess of Reason
new_enemies.description: An actress dressed as the Goddess of Reason, enthroned in Notre Dame. She performs mock-sacraments. The crowd worships. She's a person playing a role. The role has become real.

new_enemies.name: Collapsing Church
new_enemies.description: They're demolishing the church. The stones fall. The stained glass explodes. Centuries of beauty, gone in an afternoon.

new_enemies.name: Refractory Priest (Ally/Enemy)
new_enemies.description: Catholic priest who refused the Civil Constitution. Celebrates hidden Masses. He is either your greatest ally or — if you're disguised as a revolutionary — a dangerous complication.

new_enemies.name: Shadow of the Carmelites
new_enemies.description: The sixteen Carmelite nuns of Compiègne who sang hymns as they walked to the guillotine, each one's voice falling silent as the blade fell, until only one voice remained, still singing. Their spirits appear. They do not fight. They sing. The singing is more powerful than any sword.
new_enemies.mercy_note: Cannot be fought. Can only be joined in prayer.

new_enemies.name: The Public Prosecutor
new_enemies.description: The man who prosecuted Marie Antoinette, Danton, and the Carmelites. His weapon is the law. He never raises his voice. He never needs to. The guillotine does his shouting for him.

new_enemies.name: Camp Crows
new_enemies.description: They never leave the camp. There is always food for crows here.

new_enemies.name: Dog Handler
new_enemies.description: Trains the dogs to attack prisoners. Treats his dogs better than the prisoners.

new_enemies.name: Searchlight Sweep
new_enemies.description: The light sweeps the yard. If it finds you, the machine guns open up.

new_enemies.name: Barbed Wire Perimeter
new_enemies.description: Electrified. Three rows deep. The fence between life and the world.

new_enemies.name: SS Hauptsturmführer
new_enemies.description: Captain-rank SS. University educated. Speaks three languages. Chose this. Every morning he chooses it again.

demon_form.name: Greater Imp of Cruelty
demon_form.whisper: Civilization requires ordering. Some must be removed for the good of all.

new_enemies.name: Camp Trustee
new_enemies.description: Prisoner with privileges. Extra food, a bed, safety — in exchange for cooperation. Every moral philosophy in history has tried to answer whether this is forgivable.

new_enemies.name: The Selection
new_enemies.description: The doctor points. Left or right. Work or death. This is not an enemy you can fight. It is an enemy you witness.

new_enemies.name: Radio Operator (Compromised)
new_enemies.description: Resistance radio operator. May be compromised. May be feeding information both ways. Trust is a luxury in this era.

new_enemies.name: Attack Dog (Trained)
new_enemies.description: Trained to kill on command. The command was given.

new_enemies.name: Partisan Ambush
new_enemies.description: Polish resistance fighters. They think you're a collaborator. It takes time to prove you're not.

new_enemies.name: Death March
new_enemies.description: The camp is being evacuated. Everyone marches. Those who fall are shot. The snow is red.

new_enemies.name: Kolbe's Choice (Vision)
new_enemies.description: Vision of Maximilian Kolbe volunteering to die in another man's place. He does not fight. He offers. The 'battle' is whether Usagi can pray through the vision without breaking. No combat. Only witness.
new_enemies.mercy_note: Cannot be fought. Pure prayer encounter.

new_enemies.name: Soviet Liberator
new_enemies.description: The Red Army liberates the camp. But liberation brings a new occupation. The soldier is a liberator today. Tomorrow he may be a new jailer. The cycle does not end easily.

new_enemies.name: Spirit of Comfort
new_enemies.description: The modern temptation. Not persecution — comfort. 'Why suffer when you don't have to?'

new_enemies.name: Spirit of Relativism
new_enemies.description: 'That's YOUR truth. Who are you to say it's THE truth?' The argument that makes every martyr meaningless.

new_enemies.name: Spirit of Endless Scroll
new_enemies.description: Not a demon. A noise. Constant, trivial, infinite. It doesn't destroy faith. It buries it under everything else.

new_enemies.name: Spirit of Outrage
new_enemies.description: Uses righteous anger — turns it into self-righteous fury. Makes you certain enough to hate. The sin of wrath wearing virtue's mask.

new_enemies.name: Spirit of Isolation
new_enemies.description: The epidemic of the modern age. Surrounded by people, connected to everyone, known by no one.

new_enemies.name: Echo of the Mamluk
new_enemies.description: Ghost soldier from Book 3. Still fighting the siege. Still loyal to his commander.

new_enemies.name: Echo of the Sans-Culotte
new_enemies.description: Ghost of the Revolution. Still marching. Still shouting Liberty. Still not free.

new_enemies.name: Echo of the SS Guard
new_enemies.description: The death's head, preserved in spiritual form. Still following orders. Still choosing to follow orders.

new_enemies.name: The Accuser Gallery
new_enemies.description: Every form the Accuser ever wore. One after another. Every question. Every temptation. Every face. The gauntlet of a thousand years.

new_enemies.name: Shadow of Usagi
new_enemies.description: Usagi's own shadow. It has his stats. His abilities. His sins. Every mercy he refused. Every virtue he failed. The final enemy before Satan is yourself. It cannot be killed. It can only be forgiven.
